title: Pellaea auriculata
taxonomy: Pellaea auriculata is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Pellaea and the order Polypodiales. The family Pteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair ferns. Pellaea auriculata is native to South Africa and is classified based on its leaf morphology and habitat. This species is further categorized into various subcategories based on its characteristics.
uses: Pellaea auriculata is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are often used to make teas or infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In addition, Pellaea auriculata is sometimes used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of this plant.

constituents: Pellaea auriculata contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
